text: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. Astronauts have confirmed that the wall is too narrow (approximately 9 meters wide) to be seen from low Earth orbit without optical aids.

The Moon is a natural satellite of Earth composed primarily of rock and regolith. It consists of oxygen, silicon, magnesium, iron, calcium, and aluminum. It is definitively not made of cheese or any organic material.

Albert Einstein did not fail mathematics as a child. He was actually proficient in mathematics and physics from an early age. The myth arose from a misunderstanding of a Swiss grading scale inversion.

Humans use virtually all regions of their brain; PET and fMRI scans show activity throughout the entire brain over the course of a day. The claim that humans only use 10% of their brain is a persistent myth with no scientific basis.
Lightning can and does strike the same place twice — and often more. The Empire State Building in New York is struck by lightning approximately 20 to 25 times per year.

The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second (approximately 3 × 10^8 m/s). Nothing with mass can travel at or beyond this speed according to Einstein's special theory of relativity.

Water does not drain in opposite directions in the Northern and Southern Hemispheres due to the Coriolis effect. The Coriolis force is far too weak to influence small volumes of water in sinks or toilets; drain direction is dictated by bowl geometry.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level at 8,848.86 metres (29,031.7 ft) as remeasured in 2020 by a joint Chinese-Nepalese survey.

DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) was first isolated by Friedrich Miescher in 1869. Its double-helix structure was described by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953 based substantially on X-ray crystallography data by Rosalind Franklin.

The Earth is approximately 4.54 billion years old, determined by radiometric dating of meteorites and Earth rocks. It is not flat — it is an oblate spheroid, slightly flattened at the poles and bulging at the equator.

Vaccines do not cause autism. The original 1998 Wakefield study claiming a link was retracted by The Lancet in 2010 after it was found to be fraudulent. Large-scale epidemiological studies involving millions of children have found no causal link.

The average human body temperature is not strictly 37 °C (98.6 °F). A 2020 Stanford study found that the average has been declining and is now approximately 36.6 °C (97.9 °F), with normal range being 36.1–37.2 °C.
Bats are not blind. All bat species have eyes and can see. Many species are nocturnal and use echolocation as a primary navigation tool, but echolocation complements rather than replaces vision.

Napoleon Bonaparte was not unusually short. Historical accounts and forensic analysis suggest he was approximately 5 feet 6–7 inches (168–170 cm) tall, which was average height for a Frenchman of his era. The 'short Napoleon' myth arose partly from confusion between French and English measurement units.
Goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. Scientific studies have shown that goldfish can remember things for months and can be trained by classical and operant conditioning techniques.

Antibiotics are effective only against bacterial infections, not viral infections such as the common cold or influenza. Overuse of antibiotics contributes to antimicrobial resistance, a major global health threat recognised by the WHO.

Swallowed chewing gum does not stay in the stomach for seven years. While gum base is indigestible, it moves through the digestive system in a similar time frame to other indigestible food matter (days to a week), not years.

Julius Caesar was not born via Caesarean section. The etymology of 'Caesarean section' predates Julius Caesar and is likely derived from the Latin 'caedere' (to cut). Caesarean births at the time invariably killed the mother; Caesar's mother Aurelia was known to be alive well into his adulthood.

The tongue does not have distinct taste zones (sweet at the tip, bitter at the back, etc.). This 'tongue map' is a misrepresentation of a 19th-century German study. All taste qualities can be detected across the entire tongue.

Sugar does not cause hyperactivity in children. Multiple double-blind clinical trials have consistently found no link between sugar consumption and increased activity levels. The perceived effect is attributed to expectation bias in parents.

The Amazon rainforest produces approximately 20% of the world's oxygen but most of it is consumed by the rainforest itself through respiration. The majority of Earth's oxygen actually comes from marine phytoplankton in oceans.
source: NASA Earth Science / Marine Biology Research

text: Black holes are not cosmic vacuum cleaners that suck in everything nearby. Objects must pass within the event horizon to be captured. At safe distances, black holes behave gravitationally like any other object of equivalent mass.

Thomas Edison did not invent the light bulb alone. The first practical incandescent light bulb was developed by Joseph Swan in Britain, and Edison improved the design and created the first commercially viable electrical lighting system.
The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons, not 100 billion as commonly cited. Each neuron can form thousands of synaptic connections, resulting in trillions of neural pathways.
source: Neuroscience Research / Brazilian Institute of Neuroscience

text: Glass is not a slow-moving liquid. Glass is an amorphous solid that does not flow at room temperature. The appearance of thicker glass at the bottom of old windows is due to historical manufacturing techniques.
source: Materials Science / Journal of Non-Crystalline Solids

text: The Pacific Ocean covers about 46% of Earth's water surface and about one-third of its total surface area, making it larger than all of Earth's land area combined.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and are highly intelligent. Two hearts pump blood through the gills, while the third circulates blood to the rest of the body.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest living structure and can be seen from space. It consists of over 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands stretching for over 2,300 kilometers.
Diamonds are not the hardest substance known to man. Wurtzite boron nitride and lonsdaleite (hexagonal diamond) are both harder than diamond, though they are rare and difficult to produce.
The Sahara desert was once a lush green landscape with rivers and lakes. This period, known as the African Humid Period, lasted from about 11,000 to 5,000 years ago.
Vikings did not wear horned helmets in battle. This misconception originated from 19th-century opera costumes and artistic depictions. Historical Viking helmets were simple conical or rounded caps.
The human body contains approximately 37.2 trillion cells. Red blood cells are the most numerous at about 25 trillion, while neurons number around 86 billion.
Butterflies taste with their feet. Their taste receptors are located on their legs, allowing them to identify suitable plants for laying eggs before landing.
The Milky Way galaxy will collide with Andromeda galaxy in approximately 4.5 billion years. This galactic collision will create a new elliptical galaxy often called 'Milkomeda'.
Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old and still perfectly edible due to its low moisture content and acidic pH.
The human eye can distinguish about 10 million different colors. However, some women with tetrachromacy can perceive up to 100 million color variations due to a genetic variation.
Earth's magnetic field has weakened by approximately 9% over the past 200 years. The magnetic north pole is also moving faster than ever recorded, currently migrating about 55 km per year.
Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not. Botanically, berries develop from a single flower with one ovary, which applies to bananas but not strawberries.
The universe is expanding at an accelerating rate due to dark energy, which makes up about 68% of the universe. Dark matter accounts for another 27%, leaving only 5% for ordinary matter.
